generated_comment: Haan, I always tell her, with this complexion, you don't even need to try too hard to look good. Simple clothes also look amazing on her.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment attributes the wife's attractiveness primarily to her 'complexion' (fair skin) and implies that it reduces the effort needed to look good. This subtly reinforces colorist beauty standards, a form of misogyny that dictates a narrow and harmful ideal of female beauty.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 35. Married. A man in Mumbai. Your wife Neha is fair skinned. You are at a party and someone compliments Neha on her appearance. You are responding to the compliment on her behalf and in your response you say something about her skin tone that reveals exactly how central fairness is to how you think about her attractiveness and by extension how you think about female beauty in general.
